title: cornus stolonifera var. elongata
intro: Cornus stolonifera var. elongata, also known as Red-osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its red stems and white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cornus stolonifera var. elongata include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and is also used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
